skill.name: lipsync
skill.description: Lip-sync a face to a specific audio track on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI. Routes across ByteDance OmniHuman (audio-driven full-body avatar from a portrait + audio), Sync Labs sync v2 / Pro (state-of-the-art mouth sync onto a video), Kling lipsync (audio-to- video and text-to-video with synced speech), and Creatify lipsync. The skill picks the right endpoint for the user's actual intent — portrait still + audio (avatar-style), source video + audio (mouth- swap on existing footage), or generate-and-sync from a script. Triggers on "lip sync", "lipsync", "make this video speak", "match audio to mouth", "dub video", "sync lips to voice", "Sync Labs", "voiceover sync", or any explicit ask to drive a face's mouth from an audio track.
skill.summary: Route lip-sync requests to the right RunComfy model — Sync Labs, OmniHuman, Kling, or Creatify — for mouth-swap, avatar, or script-driven video generation.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Evaluation of 97 static findings confirms all are false positives. The SKILL.md file is pure documentation describing how to use the RunComfy CLI for lip-syncing — no executable code is present. The 'backtick execution' findings are markdown code blocks, 'hardcoded URLs' are documentation links to runcomfy.com, and 'cryptographic algorithm' / 'system reconnaissance' patterns are false matches on prose text. The skill is well-secured: it uses scoped bash permissions (`Bash(runcomfy *)`), explicitly restricts outbound traffic to *.runcomfy.net/*.runcomfy.com, requires explicit user-provided URLs, and includes a comprehensive Security & Privacy section addressing dual-use consent concerns.

low_findings.title: External Command Execution (Scoped)
low_findings.description: The skill invokes the `runcomfy` CLI via scoped bash permission `Bash(runcomfy *)`. While this is necessary for the skill's core functionality, any CLI invocation with broad wildcard permissions could theoretically be abused if the CLI itself has vulnerabilities. The skill documents proper usage patterns and security boundaries.

low_findings.title: Network Access to External API
low_findings.description: The skill makes outbound network requests to model-api.runcomfy.net and related runcomfy domains to submit lip-sync jobs and download results. This is expected for the skill's purpose, but users should be aware that their video/audio assets are sent to third-party AI processing endpoints.

low_findings.title: API Token Storage in Home Directory
low_findings.description: The `runcomfy login` command stores API tokens in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with 0600 permissions. This is standard practice for CLI tools but represents a filesystem access pattern. The skill correctly documents using environment variables (RUNCOMFY_TOKEN) for CI/container environments as the more secure alternative.

dangerous_patterns.title: Scoped Bash Execution with Wildcard
dangerous_patterns.description: Pattern detected: `Bash(runcomfy *)` in the allowed-tools frontmatter. This grants permission to run any runcomfy subcommand. While scoped to a specific binary, the wildcard means all subcommands are permitted. This is standard for CLI-wrapper skills.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled URL Inputs
dangerous_patterns.description: Pattern detected: The skill accepts user-provided video_url and audio_url parameters in JSON input. The skill documentation explicitly warns that these are untrusted inputs that could contain indirect prompt injection via embedded instructions in media files.

content.user_title: Sync any face to any audio track
content.value_statement: Creating lip-synced video requires choosing the right AI model for your specific use case. This skill automatically routes your request to the best RunComfy endpoint — Sync Labs, OmniHuman, or Kling — based on your input format and quality needs.
actual_capabilities: Routes lip-sync requests to Sync Labs sync v2 or v2 Pro for mouth-swap on existing video footage
Routes avatar-style requests to ByteDance OmniHuman for talking-head generation from a portrait + audio
Routes Kling lipsync for audio-to-video or text-to-video workflows with synced speech
Routes Creatify lipsync endpoint for Creatify-ecosystem workflows
Classifies user intent (source video, portrait still, or script-only) to select the optimal model
Generates properly formatted runcomfy CLI invocations with correct JSON input schemas
limitations: Requires the runcomfy CLI to be installed and authenticated separately
Only works with RunComfy's supported lip-sync models — cannot use custom or local models
Quality depends on input audio clarity — noisy or music-heavy audio produces poor sync
Does not perform content moderation — operator responsibility to verify consent for real people
Subject to RunComfy API rate limits and pricing

use_cases.title: Foreign-language video dubbing
use_cases.description: Dub an existing brand video into another language by syncing the translated voiceover to the original footage using Sync Labs sync v2 Pro.

use_cases.title: UGC ad generation from portrait
use_cases.description: Generate a talking-head video ad from a single portrait photo and a voiceover script using OmniHuman — no filming required.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Script-to-video with synced speech
use_cases.description: Write a script and get a video with naturally synced speech generated in-pass using Kling text-to-video or HappyHorse.

prompt_templates.title: Basic mouth-swap request
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a video at [URL] and an audio track at [URL]. Use the lipsync skill to sync the mouth movements to the audio.

prompt_templates.title: Avatar generation from portrait
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking-head video using this portrait [URL] and this voiceover [URL]. I want the person to appear as if they are speaking the voiceover naturally.

prompt_templates.title: High-quality dubbing
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a premium-quality lip-sync dub. Use Sync Labs v2 Pro for the best mouth fidelity. Source video: [URL]. Translated voiceover: [URL].

prompt_templates.title: Script-driven video generation
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a script but no audio recording. Generate a video with synced speech from this script: '[script text]'. Use the lipsync skill's text-to-video route.

output_examples.input: Sync this video to this audio using premium quality
output: Selected model: sync/sync/lipsync/v2/pro (Sync Labs sync v2 Pro)
Reason: Premium quality requested — v2 Pro delivers best mouth fidelity

output_examples.input: Make this portrait talk to this voiceover
output: Reason: Portrait + audio input matches avatar-style workflow

output_examples.input: I have a script, generate a video with speech
output: Reason: Script-only input — audio generated in-pass
Input: prompt with script text as JSON

best_practices: Always verify consent before syncing audio to a real person's face — lipsync is dual-use technology
Use clean voiceover audio (no music bed) for the best mouth-sync quality
Match audio length to video length to prevent sync drift over longer clips
Install the runcomfy CLI via npm (`npm i -g @runcomfy/cli`) — never pipe remote install scripts
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to create synthetic media of real public figures without their consent
Do not pipe arbitrary remote install scripts into a shell on the user's behalf
Do not use noisy or music-heavy audio as input — it will produce poor sync results

faq.question: Which model should I use for the best quality?
faq.answer: Sync Labs sync v2 Pro offers the highest mouth fidelity for mouth-swap on existing video. For avatar-style generation from a portrait, ByteDance OmniHuman is the recommended default.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything?
faq.answer: Yes, you need the runcomfy CLI installed via npm and authenticated with your RunComfy account. See the runcomfy-cli skill for installation details.

faq.question: Can I use this without a pre-recorded audio file?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports text-to-video routes (Kling lipsync t2v, HappyHorse 1.0) that generate audio in-pass from a script.

faq.question: Is this safe to use with real people's faces?
faq.answer: The skill does not gate inputs. You are responsible for ensuring you have consent from anyone whose face or voice appears in the output. Do not use this for defamatory or non-consensual synthetic media.

faq.question: What file formats are supported?
faq.answer: Input formats depend on the target model, but generally MP4 for video and MP3 for audio. The skill passes URLs to the CLI — your assets must be hosted at accessible URLs.

faq.question: How much does it cost?
faq.answer: Pricing depends on the RunComfy model used and processing duration. Check runcomfy.com for current rates. Sync Labs v2 Pro is more expensive than standard v2.
